avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes. In the regions exposed to a lot of new snow caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and. In addition the fresh wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are capable of being triggered in some locations. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In addition sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. New snow and wind slabs are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack on steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be very mild. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in all aspects above the tree line. Wind slabs require caution. In all aspects slab avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. In particular very steep sunny slopes, intermediate and high altitudes:
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.

snowpackStructure.comment:
These spring-like weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of wind from northerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, especially in all aspects above approximately 2000 m at elevated altitudes.
The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Since Monday the wind has been strong. The clearly visible wind slabs of the last few days can be released in some cases at intermediate and high altitudes, especially at their margins.

Places where persistent weak layers exist in the old snowpack, sometimes consisting of buried surface hoar, are unfavourable. Especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the weakly bonded old snow and reach medium size. This applies in particular in case of a large load.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from midday.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow fell on Monday in some regions. As a consequence of a strong northwesterly wind, hard wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.
Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The clearly visible wind slabs of the last few days can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on west to north to east facing aspects at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size. In particular places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are unfavourable. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday.

In particular very steep sunny slopes, intermediate and high altitudes: In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from midday.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong northeasterly wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed since Monday in all aspects.

In particular low and intermediate altitudes sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust.

Adjacent to ridgelines in high Alpine regions snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing consolidation of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: The more recent wind slabs represent the main danger. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size. Increase in danger of moist and wet snow slides as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.

tendency.highlights: Saturday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. This applies in particular above the tree line, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in east to north to west facing aspects. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Increase in danger of moist and wet snow slides as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported some snow. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on east to north to west facing aspects and generally above the tree line.
The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.

tendency.highlights: Saturday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads in particular.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The snowpack will be well bonded at low and intermediate altitudes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northeasterly directions, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last few days especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at elevated altitudes. These can be released, in particular by large loads and reach medium size.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from midday.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Since Monday the wind has been strong over a wide area. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

Especially in shady places that are protected from the wind high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The upper section of the snowpack is dry and its surface has a crust that is not capable of bearing a load. In very isolated cases weak layers exist in the top section of the snowpack in particular on extremely steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural activity of small and medium moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase, in particular on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

Wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases, this also applies at a distance from ridgelines, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Where the wind was less strong, soft, but not very visible, slabs formed. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.
In isolated cases the dry avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 5 to 20 cm of snow fell on Tuesday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
The solar radiation will give rise to increasing consolidation of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase.
The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the snow drift accumulations.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Winter sport participants can release avalanches only in isolated cases. They can be triggered in the old snowpack and reach medium size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes in high Alpine regions. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In addition small and medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of prolonged bright spells especially on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The gusty wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Hard wind slabs formed. These are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. The high temperatures will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
More recent wind slabs are clearly recognisable. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on steep shady slopes.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in isolated cases in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The various wind slabs have bonded with each other and the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can still be released. This applies in particular in case of a large load. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls. On steep sunny slopes moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack's surface remains generally characterized by alternating eroded areas interspersed with deposited zones; in many areas, hard surface crusts are present, locally hidden. Near the crests, evident cornices remain in many places. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some cases.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy. Continuous warming.
The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

Happy Easter!

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be stable over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The snowpack will be well bonded at low and intermediate altitudes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind from northeasterly directions, small wind slabs formed in the last few days in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. These can in some cases be released, mostly by large loads in isolated cases.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from midday.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Since Monday the wind has been strong over a wide area. The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

In shady places that are protected from the wind high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The upper section of the snowpack is dry and its surface has a crust that is not capable of bearing a load.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing consolidation of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be generally stable. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack on steep sunny slopes.
